REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1143 Palou Ave San Francisco, Ca 94124)
Off Calendar Jan-06-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sheila Robello for administrator Shaka Green (via Zoom).
Milla Lvovich for interested party Paris Harris (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Report of Sale and Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property located at 1143 Palou Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94124.

The Court noted the petition reflected an initial bid in the amount of $915,000 and further noted that a reappraisal had been recently filed.

The Court announced the first overbid amount of $961,250 and inquired whether any party wished to submit an overbid. No overbids were made.

The Court confirmed the sale of the real property for $915,000, subject to the terms and conditions set forth in the Report of Sale, and granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property. No additional bond was required.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1143 Palou Ave San Francisco, Ca 94124)
Off Calendar Nov-25-2025 Continued To Dec-09-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sheila Robello for administrator Shaka Green (via Zoom).
Milla Lvovich for interest party Paris Harris (via Zoom).
Regina Whitaker, daughter of decedent Gladys Harris (via Zoom).
Bich Loc, buyer.

The matter was called for hearing on Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property.

The Court inquired whether counsel had reviewed the Court's tentative ruling. Ms. Robello reported that the only remaining issue was the probate referee's appraisal. She stated had been communicating with the referee since November 3 but had not yet received the completed appraisal. Ms. Robello requested a brief 2-week continuance to allow time to obtain and file the appraisal, and stated that the buyer had a locked interest rate and no overbids were anticipated.

Ms. Robello agreed to address all other outstanding issues before the next hearing, such as submitting documentation clarifying that the subject property was an improved property rather than unimproved, as well as a waiver of notice for former counsel, Mr. Haas.

Ms. Lvovich stated she had no objection to the petition or Ms. Robello's request for continuance.

The Court continued the matter to December 9, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement Between Estate Administrator, Shaka Green, Regina Whitaker, And Paris Harris; For Retention Of Jurisdiction Pursuant To Ccp Section 664.6; Request For Authority To Sell Real Property Located At 1143 Palou Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94124
Off Calendar Jun-10-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Vincent Yu, Esq., for Shaka Green, Petitioner.

Makayla Domingo, Esq., for Paris Harris, Interested Party.

At the outset, the court and Mr. Yu discuss the reason for a request for authority to sell the Estate's real property when only court confirmation of the sale is required. The court advises that it intends to take the matter under submission with the intent to grant. No objection to the preceding is presented. At the court's query, Ms. Domingo confirms of the intent to file a Request for Dismissal of her client's homestead petition.

The court takes the subject matter under submission with the intent to grant. The court instructs Ms. Domingo to file the above-referenced Request for Dismissal forthwith.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Chwin. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-01-2025 Continued To Oct-07-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Andrea Lovrincevic, specially appearing for Milla Lvovich, for Paris Harris, Petitioner.
Sheila Robello, for Shaka Green, Personal Representative.

At the court's query, Ms. Robello reports that the parties have reached a settlement and expects to file a Petition for Approval of Settlement within one week. Ms. Robello reports that she expects the settlement to include resolution of the Homestead Petition and expects that the Estate should be closed by the end of this year. A mutually agreeable continuance date is discussed and confirmed.

The Status of Mediation is taken off calendar. The Status on Close of Estate and Petition for Order Setting Aside Probate Homestead are continued to Oct-07-2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Stephen Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Loke. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Jul-02-2024 Continued To Sep-17-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello appeared by zoom for Shaka Green.

The court noted that Jennifer Herlihy was not present. Mrs. Robello requested a continuance stating that they were able to get one mediation session in, that they need more information as they are too far apart, that they are conducting discovery and that they reset a second mediation session for August 14th in hopes to resolve the entire matter. Petition For Order Setting Aside Probate Homestead
Off Calendar Apr-16-2024 Continued To Jul-02-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jennifer Herlihy, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for Paris Harris, Petitioner.
Shaka Green, in person, in propria persona Personal Representative.
Virginia Whitaker, in person, daughter of Decedent.

At the outset, Mr. Green advises the court as to why he failed to appear at the last calling. Upon consideration of the preceding, the Order to Appear is taken off calendar. The court queries the parties whether they'd be amenable to participate in the court's pro bono mediation program. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. Future dates are discussed. Mr. Green and Ms. Whitaker report of ongoing efforts to hire counsel and request ample time to do so. The court asks counsel to assist with coordinating the mediation. Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed by no later than Jun-18-2024. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by Jun-21-2024. The subject matter is continued to Jul-02-2024. The Parties are to return on Jul-02-2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The parties are instructed to monitor the court's website to obtain a copy of the Minute Order Referring Case to Pro Bono Mediation.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Jaime. Not Reported.

Petition For Order Setting Aside Probate Homestead
Off Calendar Feb-27-2024 Continued To Mar-26-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Morgan Durham, Esq., for Paris Harris, Petitioner.
Eric Hass, Esq., for Shaka Green, Administrator.

The court at the outset acknowledges how uncommon the instant petition is. The court confirms with Mr. Hass of the intent to file a written objection but notes that he is waiting to file it as the parties have agreed to mediation, and a mediator. Ms. Durham confirms Mr. Hass' testimony.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Mar-26-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Mr. Hass is instructed to file a written objection to the instant petition, forthwith.

Judge: Hon. Ronald Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Maloney. Not Reported.
